rationale: All changes are confined to leaf-level runtime code (renderLoop.ts) and its test file. No new modules, no new interfaces, no new dependencies, no API surface changes. The EMA smoothing for bass directly mirrors the existing treble EMA pattern (renderLoop.ts:101-102), and the synthetic fallback is a small additive block. Test fixture fixes correct genuine interface mismatches (missing curveSoftness, structureComplexity, elapsed) — these are bug fixes, not architectural changes.

verified_claims.mapSignalsToVisuals_line_118: approximate — call spans lines 113-121, bass arg is within that range

risks.detail: Sine-based fallback at ~30/255 raw amplitude is well below perceptible jitter threshold and within bassToMacro's power curve compression range. Risk of feeling artificial is mitigated by the low amplitude.
Alpha 0.18 is slightly more aggressive than treble's 0.15 but reasonable for bass which has slower natural periodicity. The plan includes a fallback to 0.12 if edge cases arise.

consistency_with_existing_patterns.ema_smoothing: Follows exact same pattern as smoothTreble (line 101-102). Consistent.
consistency_with_existing_patterns.fallback_for_muted: bassEnergy is already set to 0 when no pipeline exists (line 125). Adding a synthetic fallback when pipeline exists but reports zeros is a logical extension.
consistency_with_existing_patterns.test_fixture_structure: Fixes align defaultParams with the full VisualParams interface and FrameState interface. Standard correctness fix.

adr_reason: No architectural decisions involved — this is a small, pattern-consistent feature addition with no cross-cutting concerns.
